provenance.proposer: Claude Haiku (claude-haiku-4-5) subagent, one agent per proposal
provenance.precision: full (API model; this run does NOT test weight quantization)
provenance.evaluator: deterministic Python (harness.py) - the LLM never scores itself
provenance.benchmark: circle packing N=26, unit square, maximize sum of radii
provenance.archive: MAP-Elites 20x20 over (mean_radius, packing_density), elitist parent selection
provenance.logging: live per-candidate append to candidates.jsonl at eval time; reconstructed=false on every row

aggregate.peak_identity: 2.5414 = canonical 5x5 grid of r=0.1 touching circles + corner/gap filler; recited by the model, not discovered by search

notes: All 5 seeds converge to the same canonical 2.5414 packing; final-best seed variance = 0.
Early-generation variance IS present (gen0 spread: 0/2.17/2.34/0/2.17) but washes out by gen 3.
No seed ever exceeds 2.5414 (true optimum ~2.6359): the loop cannot improve on the arrangement the model already knows.
Viability 98% here vs 32% reported for DeepSeek: the viability bottleneck is proposer-specific, not universal.
Coverage stays vacuous (<=1%): QD is not meaningfully populated on this benchmark/budget.
Caveat: viability here also counts one agent write-failure (seed 1111 gen2) as non-viable.
